text: {"claims":[{"statement":"Chicken eggs are an affordable source of protein and other nutrients.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"Chicken eggs are an affordable source of protein and other nutrients.","entailment":"The quoted sentence directly states that chicken eggs provide affordable protein and other nutrients.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"Eggs are naturally high in cholesterol.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"They're also naturally high in cholesterol.","entailment":"The quoted sentence directly asserts that eggs contain a high amount of cholesterol naturally.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"The cholesterol in eggs does not seem to raise cholesterol levels the way foods high in trans fats and saturated fats do.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"the cholesterol in eggs doesn't seem to raise cholesterol levels the way some other foods, such as those high in trans fats and saturated fats, do.","entailment":"The quoted sentence compares the effect of egg cholesterol to that of trans‑fat and saturated‑fat foods, indicating a weaker effect on blood cholesterol levels.","population":"","intervention":"consumption of egg cholesterol","comparator":"consumption of foods high in trans fats and saturated fats","outcome":"raise cholesterol levels","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Some studies have found a link between eating eggs and heart disease.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Although some studies have found a link between eating eggs and heart disease,","entailment":"The quoted sentence reports that research has observed an association between egg consumption and heart disease.","population":"","intervention":"eating eggs","comparator":"","outcome":"heart disease","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Foods commonly eaten with eggs, such as bacon, sausage and ham, might do more to boost heart disease risk than eggs do.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"The foods people typically eat with eggs, such as bacon, sausage and ham, might do more to boost heart disease risk than eggs do.","entailment":"The quoted sentence suggests that accompanying foods may have a greater impact on heart disease risk than the eggs themselves.","population":"","intervention":"consumption of bacon, sausage, ham with eggs","comparator":"consumption of eggs alone","outcome":"heart disease risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"The way eggs and other foods are cooked, especially if fried in oil or butter, might play more of a role in the increased risk of heart disease than eggs themselves do.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"the way eggs and other foods are cooked, especially if fried in oil or butter, might play more of a role in the increased risk of heart disease than eggs themselves do.","entailment":"The quoted sentence attributes greater importance to cooking method (fried in oil or butter) for heart disease risk compared with the eggs themselves.","population":"","intervention":"fried cooking of eggs/foods","comparator":"non‑fried preparation of eggs","outcome":"increased risk of heart disease","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Most healthy people can eat up to seven eggs a week without increasing their risk of heart disease.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Most healthy people can eat up to seven eggs a week without increasing their risk of heart disease.","entailment":"The quoted sentence reports that, for healthy individuals, consuming up to seven eggs weekly does not raise heart disease risk.","population":"healthy people","intervention":"up to seven eggs per week","comparator":"","outcome":"risk of heart disease","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"up to seven eggs a week","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Egg consumption at the level of up to seven eggs a week might help prevent certain types of stroke.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Some studies have shown that this level of egg consumption might even help prevent certain types of stroke","entailment":"The quoted sentence indicates that studies suggest a possible protective effect of that egg intake on stroke.","population":"","intervention":"up to seven eggs a week","comparator":"","outcome":"prevention of certain types of stroke","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"up to seven eggs a week","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Egg consumption at the level of up to seven eggs a week might help prevent macular degeneration, a serious eye condition that can lead to blindness.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"and a serious eye condition called macular degeneration that can lead to blindness.","entailment":"The quoted phrase links egg consumption to a potential reduction in risk of macular degeneration, which can cause blindness.","population":"","intervention":"up to seven eggs a week","comparator":"","outcome":"macular degeneration","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"up to seven eggs a week","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"For people with diabetes, some research suggests that eating seven eggs a week increases heart disease risk.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"if you have diabetes, some research suggests that eating seven eggs a week increases heart disease risk.","entailment":"The quoted sentence reports an observed association between weekly egg intake and higher heart disease risk in diabetic individuals.","population":"people with diabetes","intervention":"eating seven eggs a week","comparator":"","outcome":"heart disease risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"seven eggs a week","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Other research failed to find a connection between eating seven eggs a week and heart disease risk in diabetics.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"However, other research failed to find the same connection.","entailment":"The quoted sentence indicates that some studies did not observe the previously mentioned association.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Some research suggests that eating eggs might increase the risk of developing diabetes.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Still other research suggests that eating eggs might increase the risk of developing diabetes in the first place.","entailment":"The quoted sentence reports a possible association between egg consumption and higher incidence of diabetes.","population":"","intervention":"eating eggs","comparator":"","outcome":"risk of developing diabetes","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Health experts now suggest eating as little dietary cholesterol as possible, aiming to keep intake under 300 milligrams a day.","claimType":"value","quote":"Health experts now suggest eating as little dietary cholesterol as you can, aiming to keep intake under 300 milligrams (mg) a day.","entailment":"The quoted sentence conveys a recommendation from health professionals regarding dietary cholesterol limits.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"under 300 milligrams a day","modality":"normative"},{"statement":"One large egg contains about 186 milligrams of cholesterol, all of which is found in the yolk.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"One large egg has about 186 mg of cholesterol, all of which is found in the yolk.","entailment":"The quoted sentence provides a factual measurement of cholesterol content in a large egg.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"about 186 mg per sentence suggests that, in low‑cholesterol diets, consuming one egg daily may be acceptable based on study findings.","population":"","intervention":"eating up to an egg a day","comparator":"","outcome":"acceptability/OK choice for cholesterol intake","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"up to an egg a day","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Egg whites contain no cholesterol but still contain protein.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"Egg whites contain no cholesterol but still contain protein.","entailment":"The quoted sentence states the factual composition of egg whites regarding cholesterol and protein.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"definitional"}]}
